articles.title: Telangana scorches at 46.5 degrees C, parts of Hyderabad record 43.4 degrees C - Telangana Today
articles.full_text:
Home |Telangana |Telangana Scorches At 46 5 Degrees C Parts Of Hyderabad Record 43 4 Degrees C
Telangana scorches at 46.5 degrees C, parts of Hyderabad record 43.4 degrees C
Telangana continues to face an intense heatwave, with Munagala in Suryapet district recording 46.5°C for the second day in a row. Hyderabad too witnessed soaring temperatures, with Karwan touching 43.4°C, while several rural districts reported even higher readings.
Hyderabad: Telangana is caught in the unrelenting grip of a severe heatwave, which for the second consecutive day touched 46.5 degrees Celsius in Munagala, Suryapet on Thursday, according to Telangana State Development Planning Society (TSDPS) data.
The State’s capital too felt the brunt of the heatwave as Karwan recorded the city’s highest reading at 43.4 degrees Celsius, between 8.30 am and 4.30 pm on Thursday. The weather data from TSDPS and independent weather trackers confirmed that rural districts are bearing the brunt of the heatwave.

articles.title: Telangana CM urges people to stay indoors amid heatwave - Dailyhunt
articles.full_text:
Hyderabad: Following the IMD (India Meteorological Department) warning of severe heatwave conditions in Telangana, Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy appealed to people not to venture out of their homes and avoid travelling during noon hours.
He directed the officials concerned to take all necessary precautionary measures and remain alert.
Chief Secretary K Ramakrishna Rao will monitor the situation in the affected districts and issue necessary guidelines to the District Collectors for the safety of people during summer. The authorities were ordered to ensure the availability of adequate ORS packets, supply of drinking water and uninterrupted power.
The Chief Minister directed the Medical and Health department to make arrangements and provide better medical treatment to the patients suffering from sunstroke and admitted to the hospitals. Local authorities were instructed to set up Chali Vendralu (free drinking water centres).
